cases.description: Sum of pairwise products across two equal-sized arrays
With only one array argument, SUMPRODUCT just sums its elements
Classic double-unary-minus idiom coerces a boolean array to 1/0 to count matches without an extra helper column
cases.expected_note: A3,A4,A5 satisfy >2; --(...) turns TRUE/FALSE into 1/0 and SUMPRODUCT sums them

cases.description: Multiplying a boolean condition array by a value array replicates SUMIF without a separate criteria function
cases.expected_note: Rows where A>2 (rows 3,4,5) contribute their B value: 30+40+50=120

cases.description: Arrays of different dimensions cannot be multiplied element-wise -> #VALUE!
cases.expected_note: Microsoft docs: array arguments must have the same dimensions, otherwise SUMPRODUCT returns #VALUE!
